title: braya oxycarpa var. scharnhorstii
intro: Braya oxycarpa var. scharnhorstii, also known as scharnhorst's braya, is a rare and endangered plant species that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to the Arctic tundra of North America, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves and roots, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and its health benefits may include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and possibly treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Braya oxycarpa var. scharnhorstii is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
